posture: Quiet Friday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a–§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Recurring or undated items were again reviewed and excluded: the NSW FOGO mandates (commercial source-separated organics from 1 July 2026; household FOGO service mandate to 2030) are regulatory-rollout dates rather than fresh innovation signals; Hawkesbury City Council's ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial and its associated FOGO deferral (to 1 July 2029) rest on prior-period decisions; and the NSW AI-in-planning program (AI Solutions Panel; $5.6m investment with $2.7m Early Adopter Grants to 16 councils; Spatial Digital Twin) produced no new dated development today. Awards-cycle administration was reviewed but not logged as an innovation signal: the opening of nominations for the Australian AI Awards 2026 (16 July) is a general cross-economy awards call, not a council-transferable adopt/trial/watch item. Overseas and network material (Bloomberg CityLab 2026 Madrid readouts, the 2025–26 Mayors Challenge winners, the Bloomberg–Johns Hopkins Mayors AI Forum, LGIU/LocalGov content) was out-of-window, network/policy content, or already logged as background. Drone items surfaced (Transport for NSW 'drone-in-a-box' traffic-response units on the M1/Hume; the PolAir-rural six-month police trial) are state-run road-operations or law-enforcement functions and fall outside the §3b council-function scope. Today is a Friday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
